{"query":"Alien: Romulus sequel director 2025 replacement search","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1524,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://variety.com/2025/film/news/alien-romulus-sequel-fede-alvarez-not-returning-1236509609/","title":"'Alien Romulus' Sequel: Director Fede Alvarez Not Returning","content":"Fede Alvarez, who reinvigorated the “Alien” franchise on the big screen last year with “Alien: Romulus,” won’t be returning for the sequel, he revealed.
Speaking to TooFab, Alvarez said he will stay on as a producer for the “Alien: Romulus” sequel, but he and franchise creator Ridley Scott will search for a new director.
“I’m going to produce it, with Ridley Scott.
Scott directed the original classic “Alien” in 1979 before passing the reins to James Cameron for its beloved sequel, “Aliens” in 1986."},{"url":"https://nexuspointnews.com/exclusive-michael-sarnoski-eyed-to-direct-the-alien-romulus-sequel/","title":"EXCLUSIVE: Michael Sarnoski Eyed to Direct the ‘Alien: Romulus’ Sequel","content":"EXCLUSIVE: Michael Sarnoski (A Quiet Place: Day One) is being considered to direct the highly anticipated sequel to Alien: Romulus, Nexus Point News has learned.
Michael Sarnoski is best known for the acclaimed drama Pig, starring Nicolas Cage, in addition to A Quiet Place: Day One.
His next film, The Death of Robin Hood, is scheduled to release in theaters later this year.
Sarnoski is also attached to direct the upcoming film adaptation of the video game Death Stranding."},{"url":"https://www.yahoo.com/entertainment/movies/articles/alien-romulus-sequel-may-found-202700114.html","title":"Alien: Romulus Sequel May Have Found Fede Álvarez’s Replacement Director","content":"The plotline and cast details for the Alien: Romulus sequel are currently undisclosed, with Disney's 20th Century Fox President of Production confirming that the movie will feature aliens and horror set pieces.
The 2024 movie Alien: Romulus was directed by Fede Álvarez, who announced in September 2025 that he wouldn't be returning to direct the sequel.
Nexus Point News states that some sources have confirmed that Michael Sarnoski is the potential candidate to take over the job after Fede Álvarez.
Additionally, Disney's 20th Century Fox President of Production, Steve Asbell, announced in October 2024 that the sequel was in the works."},{"url":"https://www.avpgalaxy.net/alien-movies/alien-romulus-2/","title":"Alien: Romulus 2 - Release Date, Cast, Plot & Rumours","content":"Fede Alvarez has confirmed he won’t be directing the film, but he has written the script with his writing partner, Rodo Sayagues.
Fede Alvarez mentioned in June 2025 that he and his co-writer Rodo Sayagues are currently writing the script and think the film will be in pre-production by October 2025.
In September 2025, Fede Alvarez confirmed he won’t be directing the film, and he and Ridley Scott are searching for a director.
No cast members have been confirmed yet, but it is expected that Cailee Spaeny and David Jonsson will reprise their roles as Rain and synthetic Andy."},{"url":"https://www.ign.com/articles/alien-romulus-director-fede-alvarez-will-produce-but-not-direct-the-sequel","title":"Alien: Romulus Director Fede Alvarez Will Produce But Not Direct the Sequel - IGN","content":"Fede Alvarez will not direct the sequel to his hit 2024 film Alien: Romulus.
Cailee Spaeny and David Jonsson are expected to reprise their respective roles from Alien: Romulus for the sequel, which will be released by 20th Century Studios."}]}

Not to be confused with *Aliens* (film) \"Aliens (film)\"). ***Alien*** is a 1979 science fiction horror film directed by Ridley Scott and written by Dan O'Bannon, based on a story by O'Bannon and Ronald Shusett. It received a wide release on June 22 and was released on September 6 in the United Kingdom. In subsequent years, *Alien* was critically reassessed and described as one of the greatest and most influential science fiction and horror films of all time.
